description: Go module init and project directory structure created
steps: Run go mod init github.com/saad039/sloplang
Create all package directories: cmd/slop, pkg/lexer, pkg/parser, pkg/codegen, pkg/runtime, examples
Verify go build ./... succeeds with empty packages

description: SlopValue struct with NewSlopValue, IsTruthy, and StdoutWrite
steps: Define SlopValue struct with Elements []any and Keys []string
Implement NewSlopValue(...any) constructor
Implement IsTruthy() returning true for non-empty Elements
Implement StdoutWrite that prints strings raw and arrays formatted
Unit tests pass for all constructor variants, IsTruthy, and StdoutWrite

description: Lexer tokenizes all Phase 1 constructs correctly
steps: Define TokenType enum and Token struct
Implement scanner with NextToken() and Tokenize()
Tokenize number literals: int, uint (u suffix), float (decimal point)
Tokenize string literals with escape handling
Tokenize operators: =, |>, [, ], comma
Skip // comments and whitespace with line tracking
Tokenize identifiers and keywords (true, false)
Unit tests pass for assignments, stdout writes, number types, comments, multiline

description: Parser produces correct AST for assignments and stdout writes
steps: Define AST node interfaces and types (Program, AssignStmt, StdoutWriteStmt, ArrayLiteral, NumberLiteral, StringLiteral, Identifier)
Implement recursive descent parser with Parse() entry point
Parse assignment: IDENT ASSIGN expr -> AssignStmt
Parse stdout write: PIPE_GT expr -> StdoutWriteStmt
Parse array literal: LBRACKET (expr COMMA...)* RBRACKET -> ArrayLiteral
Parse number, string, and identifier expressions
Unit tests pass for all statement/expression types, empty arrays, mixed arrays, error cases

description: Codegen produces valid Go source via go/ast lowering
steps: Implement Generator with Generate(*Program) returning formatted Go source
Build go/ast.File with package main, sloprt import, and main() func
Lower AssignStmt to := with sloprt.NewSlopValue call
Lower StdoutWriteStmt to sloprt.StdoutWrite() call
Lower number/string/array literals to correct Go AST nodes
Format output with go/format.Node
Unit tests pass verifying generated Go compiles and has correct structure

description: CLI reads .slop file and writes transpiled .go output
steps: Implement main() in cmd/slop/main.go
Read input file, run lexer -> parser -> codegen pipeline
Write output .go file alongside input
Handle and report errors at each stage

description: hello.slop transpiles, compiles, and runs producing correct stdout
steps: Create examples/hello.slop with x = [1, 2, 3] and |> "hello world"
E2E test lexes, parses, generates Go source
E2E test writes generated code to temp dir with go.mod replace directive
E2E test runs go build on generated code successfully
E2E test runs compiled binary and captures stdout
E2E test asserts stdout equals 'hello world' with trailing newline
